page: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 1952 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 30.5 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and memory-intensive capabilities. The instance achieved a strong multi-core score of 16753.0 on Geekbench, with notable performance in software compilation at 41131.0 and ray tracing at 61066.0. Passmark testing highlights strong database operations at 26390.07 and a strong CPU encryption score of 53228.85. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 3306.5 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 151.05 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Additionally, the server demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference text generation using a medium 7B model, reaching 27.68 tokens per second.

Given its massive memory capacity relative to the vCPU count, this instance offers high resource density for workloads requiring large memory footprints without needing proportional scaling of compute cores. This qualitative resource balance makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications. The server is ideally suited for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, software compilation, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers an exceptional memory-to-core ratio of 30.5 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, particularly in multi-core processing, database operations, and software compilation. The instance achieves a strong Passmark database score of 26390.07 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 16753.0. It also demonstrates strong performance in medium-model LLM text generation. This high memory density makes the instance highly efficient for memory-bound workloads without over-provisioning compute resources. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and intensive data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM, built for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding database workloads.
